description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese torii gate, typically found at the entrance to Shinto shrines. It features two tall cylindrical red pillars supporting a pair of horizontal crossbars, with the uppermost bar slightly curved and finished in a dark, stone-like texture. The base of each pillar is encased in a black cylindrical footing, suggesting durability and stability. The structure is minimalist, with no visible inscriptions or plaques, and its clean lines and bold colors make it a striking landmark. The torii's design is iconic, symbolizing the transition from the mundane to the sacred. Its materials and proportions evoke a sense of historical and cultural significance.
description: A traditional Japanese torii gate with red pillars, black bases, and a curved dark top crossbar, symbolizing a sacred entrance.
description_view_0: Front view showing both red pillars, black bases, and the full width of the curved dark top crossbar.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the gate, with the crossbars and pillars aligned in a straight line.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the red pillars, black bases, and curved top crossbar.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and alignment of the crossbars and pillars.
